title: Softmax-tail fused tile path
hypothesis: Introduce a softmax-specialized tile path that reduces descriptor traffic and staging overhead for imported softmax-tail workloads, with mapper support for the new fused legality and scheduling pattern.

direct_comparison.primary_question: Does fused terminal output improve the softmax-tail path on the fixed nm1 softmax baseline?
exclude: fp16_nm2_softmax_r4 during the first-stage fusion proof
broad architecture ranking across nm1 and nm2 in the first evaluation
follow_on_broad_sweep: reintroduce nm2 only after the nm1 fusion effect is established or remains ambiguous

expected_benefit: lower end-to-end latency on imported softmax-tail workloads
reduced descriptor and memory-traffic overhead relative to current flat softmax-integrated points
clearer architectural signal for whether softmax-specific compute is worth area cost
risks: requires mapper legality and lowering changes before the architecture is evaluable
could increase area enough to erase latency gains
may improve only the softmax-tail model set and not generalize to broader workloads
could degrade terminal output quality even if latency and PPA improve
